expected_properties: Outputs y and Co follow combinational logic for each input change without spurious glitches beyond propagation delay
No hidden state influences successive stimulus evaluations
additional_cases.goal: Validate that rapid successive input changes produce correct combinational results for each stimulus vector
notes: Stimulus sequence alternates between extreme values to stress combinational path
preconditions: All input ports a, b, Cin are driven for each stimulus step
stimulus_intent: Apply a sequence of alternating max (0xFFFF) and min (0x0000) values on a and b, toggling Cin each step
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after each input vector stabilizes; no clock required

expected_properties: When inputs contain unknown (X) or high‑impedance (Z) values, the module propagates X/Z to outputs according to Verilog combinational semantics
No illegal internal latch or storage is inferred
additional_cases.goal: Check module robustness to undefined input values
notes: Even though the contract has no illegal input constraints, exercising X/Z helps confirm pure combinational behavior
preconditions: Drive X or Z on any subset of a, b, Cin
stimulus_intent: Introduce X/Z on a, b, or Cin while keeping other inputs at defined values
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs immediately after input stabilization; no timing guarantees required

expected_properties: Output y equals a + b + Cin for all driven legal values
Carry-out Co reflects overflow of the 16‑bit addition
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Exercise representative legal input combinations and verify functional correctness against a reference model
notes: Adds reference‑model comparison and random stimulus to broaden basic coverage
stimulus_intent: Drive a mix of random, sequential, and corner‑case patterns on a, b, and Cin
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after input stabilization; no cycle‑accurate timing assumed

expected_properties: Carry propagation chain is correctly handled for max‑value operands
Co is asserted only when the sum exceeds 16 bits
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Exercise boundary‑value and width‑sensitive input patterns, especially carry‑chain extremes
notes: Adds explicit carry‑propagation scenarios to strengthen edge coverage
stimulus_intent: Use zero‑like, one‑like, all‑ones, and single‑bit‑carry patterns on a and b, varying Cin to test overflow
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after input stabilization; avoid assumptions about internal latency

planning_notes: Baseline cases preserved and enriched with additional functional checks while respecting contract ambiguity.
No new ports or timing guarantees were invented; all stimuli use only a, b, Cin, y, Co.
